prose:
Fukui reactivity indices computed for formaldehyde (CH₂O, 4 atoms) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (PySCF RKS, tier 'standard'), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The N+1 (anion) and N−1 (cation) states used charge ±1 and multiplicity 2. Partial charges were computed via the Mulliken scheme (PySCF). Geometry was taken directly from the user-supplied .xyz file (no re-optimization). All three SCFs converged; integrity.trustworthy = true; no warnings reported; both Fukui charge-conservation checks passed (Σf⁺ = 1.0000, Σf⁻ = 1.0000).

Per-atom Fukui indices:
  Atom 1 (O):  f⁺ = 0.46814, f⁻ = 0.46742, f⁰ = 0.46778, dual = +0.00071
  Atom 2 (C):  f⁺ = 0.70839, f⁻ = 0.11244, f⁰ = 0.41042, dual = +0.59595
  Atom 3 (H):  f⁺ = −0.08827, f⁻ = 0.21008, f⁰ = 0.06090, dual = −0.29835
  Atom 4 (H):  f⁺ = −0.08826, f⁻ = 0.21006, f⁰ = 0.06090, dual = −0.29832

Most electrophilic site: C (atom 2), f⁺ = 0.70839 (headline value).
Most nucleophilic site: O (atom 1), f⁻ = 0.46742.

The carbonyl carbon is the dominant electrophilic site (large f⁺ and positive dual descriptor), consistent with its known susceptibility to nucleophilic attack. Oxygen is the primary nucleophilic site. No warnings were issued by the engine.
